channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Casey Anthony joins TikTok and Substack to ‘advocate’ for herself
item.description: More than a decade after Casey Anthony was accused of murdering her daughter in one of the country’s most notorious murder cases, this weekend she emerged on TikTok to reintroduce herself.
item.dc:creator: Eve Upton-Clark, Fast Company

item.title: Minnesota considers blocking 'nudify' apps that use AI to make explicit images without consent
item.description: ST. PAUL, Minn. (AP) — Molly Kelly was stunned to discover in June that someone she knew had used widely available “nudification” technology to create highly realistic and sexually explicit videos and images of her, using family photos that were posted on social media.
item.dc:creator: STEVE KARNOWSKI, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: El CIO ante el desafío de gestionar los agentes de IA
item.description: En un escenario en el que los agentes de IA ya están preparados para hacerse cargo de gran parte del flujo de trabajo de las empresas, los líderes de TI tendrán que afrontar un reto cada vez más complejo: gestionarlos.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: 2025 global network outage report and internet health check
item.description: The reliability of services delivered by ISPs, cloud providers and conferencing services is critical for enterprise organizations.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Employee screening data breach exposes 3.3 million records
item.description: Major employee screening company data breach affects 3.3 million people. Tech expert Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson explains what happened and gives five ways to protect your data.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Popular female streamers are bringing on security after stars were attacked during an IRL stream
item.description: Female streamers are being told to hire security after a spate of recent attacks. Popular Twitch stars Valkyrae, Cinna, and Emiru were out in public at the Santa Monica Pier on March 2 as part of their “sisathon” streaming marathon, when a man lunged toward them, saying, “I’ll kill you right now.
item.dc:creator: Eve Upton-Clark, Fast Company

item.title: Sony is now issuing PS Plus compensation following the recent PSN outage
item.description: PlayStation Plus subscribers are officially receiving compensation following the PSN outage last month.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Uber teams up with Waymo to start selling driverless rides in Austin, Texas
item.description: Uber is shifting into a new gear in Austin, Texas where its ride-hailing service will begin dispatching self-driving cars to pick up passengers looking for a way to get around that city.
item.dc:creator: Michael Liedtke, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Electronic employee monitoring reaches an all-time high
item.description: Monitoring of both remote and in-office employees is at an all-time high, a trend highlighted by federal workers being told to report their weekly accomplishments. A study by the Massachusetts Institute of Technology (MIT) found that 80% of companies are monitoring remote or hybrid workers.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Why genAI-powered intelligent document processing is a big deal
item.description: One of the earliest digital transformation drivers was digitalization, in which organizations converted paper-based processes to digitized workflows. In ideal circumstances, paper documents became web or mobile forms, and workflows replaced handoffs.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: How Block is accelerating engineering velocity through developer experience
item.description: The Block ecosystem of brands – including Square, Cash App, Spiral and TIDAL – is driven by more than 4,000 engineers and thousands of interconnected software systems. Today, Block is doubling down on engineering velocity, investing in major initiatives to help teams ship software even faster.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: The Battle Over Automated Traffic Cameras in Georgia | Speeding Tickets, Revenue, and School Safety
item.description: The debate over automated traffic cameras in Georgia heats up with lawmakers introducing bills to ban or reform the use of speed cameras, citing concerns over revenue generation and safety.
item.dc:creator: Jeff Amy, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Hulu ended its first live Oscars stream before the final awards. Viewers were not happy
item.description: Sunday night marked Hulu’s first time airing the Oscars live. But its Academy Awards debut ended on a chaotic and frustratingly premature note.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: Tariffs an 'expensive game to play': Powerteam International CEO
item.description: Tariffs are going to be an "expensive game to play," Bill Walsh, CEO and founder of Powerteam International, said.
item.dc:creator: Connell McShane, NewsNation

item.title: More Americans watch YouTube on TV than on their smartphones—here’s what that means for creators and viewers
item.description: YouTube celebrated its 20th birthday last month. Currently, users watch more than a billion hours of content on the Google-owned video platform every day. And increasingly, that content is streamed on TVs rather than smartphones.
item.dc:creator: Yasmin Gagne, Fast Company

item.title: UK data protection watchdog investigating how TikTok uses children's personal data
item.description: LONDON (AP) — The U.K.'s data protection watchdog said Monday that it's investigating how TikTok uses the personal information of teenagers to deliver content recommendations to them when they use the social media platform.
item.dc:creator: SYLVIA HUI, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Breaking up Intel is the ‘dumbest idea’ around: Former CEO Craig Barett
item.description: In a blistering critique, former Intel CEO and Chairman Craig Barrett has strongly opposed breaking the company into separate design and foundry units, arguing that Intel’s recent technological resurgence positions it to challenge TSMC’s dominance in the semiconductor industry without corporate dism
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Download the UEM vendor comparison chart, 2025 edition
item.description: Unified endpoint management (UEM) is a strategic IT approach that consolidates how enterprises secure and manage an array of deployed devices including phones, tablets, PCs, and even IoT devices.
item.dc:creator: us

item.title: Who needs Google technology? Probably not you
item.description: Clearly Google is doing something right. Although Google Cloud’s revenue still lags AWS and Microsoft Azure, it’s growing much faster (albeit on a smaller base). But that’s not the real story of its growth. The story is that it’s growing at all.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Two AI developer strategies: Hire engineers or let AI do the work
item.description: The stark difference in the way tech giants in China and the US are approaching AI for internal operations was illustrated late this week by separate announcements from Salesforce and Alibaba.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Chip Ganassi Racing partners with OpenAI in first motorsports venture for AI company
item.description: ST. PETERSBURG, Fla. (AP) — Chip Ganassi Racing on Friday said it has partnered on a strategic research collaboration with OpenAI — a deal that makes the two-time reigning IndyCar championship winning team the first to bring in a partner from the artificial intelligence field.
item.dc:creator: JENNA FRYER, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: AI & Data Exchange 2025: GAO’s Lindsey Saul on AI helping speed data analytics, improve oversight
item.description: AI presents opportunities for greater efficiency, but GAO chief data officer says its effectiveness hinges on data quality. The post AI & Data Exchange 2025: GAO’s Lindsey Saul on AI helping speed data analytics, improve oversight first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Jory Heckman, Federal News Network

item.title: One Tech Tip: Getting a lot of unwanted phone calls? Here are ways to stop them
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Unwanted phone calls are out of control. Whether it's a robocall trying to sell you something or spam calls from scammers trying to rip you off, it's enough to make you want to stop answering your phone.
item.dc:creator: KELVIN CHAN, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: AI & Data Exchange 2025: LexisNexis’ Jake Nelson on potential of genAI to support legal services
item.description: Generative AI tools can help agencies’ legal teams tackle repetitive tasks, LexisNexis’ general manager of emerging products says. The post AI & Data Exchange 2025: LexisNexis’ Jake Nelson on potential of genAI to support legal services first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Jory Heckman, Federal News Network

item.title: StarlingX bridges IPv4-IPv6 gap with dual-stack networking support
item.description: The Open Infrastructure Foundation is out with the release of StarlingX 10.0, a significant update to the open-source distributed cloud platform designed for IoT, 5G, O-RAN and edge computing applications.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Vietnam plans energy shift toward building more solar, less reliance on gas and coal
item.description: HANOI, Vietnam (AP) — Vietnam is revising its energy plans to focus more on large solar farms and less on reliance on coal and natural gas. The fast-growing economy now aims to get 16% of its energy from the sun — more than triple its earlier target of just 5%.
item.dc:creator: ANIRUDDHA GHOSAL, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Elon Musk’s SpaceX prepares for 8th Starship launch, pending FAA approval
item.description: SpaceX is slated to launch its Starship for the eighth time from Boca Chica, Texas, Friday, though plans could be thwarted if the FAA fails to give the green light.
item.dc:creator: Fox News
